Q7 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and answer the following question.
Among Nature’s most intriguing phenomena are the partnerships formed by many different species. The name used for these relationships, Symbiosis, comes from Greek meaning “living together”. Not all symbiotic relationships are the same. There are some called commensal relationships, in which one partner gains a benefit while the other gains little or none but is not
harmed. One example is the relationship between two types of fish – remoras and sharks. The remora, which is long and often striped, attaches itself to a shark (sometimes to another type of fish or a whale), using a sucker on its head. When the shark makes a kill, the hitchhiker briefly detaches itself to feed on the scraps. Another type of symbiotic relationship is parasitism, in which one partner benefits at the expense of others. Ticks and tapeworms are among familiar parasites.
The third type of symbiotic relationship, called mutualism, is a true partnership in which both partners benefit. The relationship may be limited as when zebras and wildebeest graze together on the vast African grasslands. Each species can survive on its own, but together their chances of detecting predators are improved because each contributes a specially keen sense. (Zebras have
the better eyesight; wildebeest, hearing and sense of smell). In a few cases, partners are so interdependent that one cannot survive without the other. Most mutualistic relationships probably lie somewhere in between.
The passage talks about how animals

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The passage talks about symbiosis i.e. how animals live together.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following flow diagram for live variable analysis:

 
 
Find the live range for variable a.
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In block 1, a is assigned 0 and that value is used in block 2 so 1 → 2 a is alive.
In block 4, a is reassigned some value so 2 → 3 → 4 a is dead and 4 → 5 → 2 again a is alive.
So option (c) is correct.

Q25 MCQ Not checked

Not attempted

Which of the following states is shared by the parent process and the child process when a process uses the fork( ) procedure to create a new process?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The parent process and the recently split child process only share the shared memory segments.
The Kernel is unaware of user-level threads, but it is aware of Kernel threads.
For the newly generated process, copies of the heap and stack are constructed.

Q33 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : In paging, a trap is raised if the virtual page number is present in the page table.
S2 : In paging, a trap is raised if the access type (read/write/execute) conflicts with the protection bits in the page table entry.
S3 : In paging, a trap is raised if the page table entry exists but its valid bit indicates the page is not in main memory.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• If the virtual page number is present in the page table and valid, translation succeeds — no trap is raised. So, S1 is incorrect.
(A trap is raised only when it is not present or invalid).
• Protection bits control allowed access. If the process violates them (e.g., write to read-only
page), a protection fault trap occurs. So, S2 is correct.
• Valid entry but valid bit = 0 → page is on disk, not in RAM → page fault trap. So, S3 is correct.

Solution

So, S2 is correct.

Q43 NAT Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the partially ordered set (P(S), ⊇) where S = {1, 2, 3, 4, ....., 256}. Let A, B, C and D be subsets of S such that A contains all multiples of 8, and B contains multiples of 12, C consists of multiples of 18 and D consists of all perfect squares respectively.
Then the cardinality of LUB(LUB(A, B), LUB(B, C)) is equal to ________.

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3
In case of the superset relation, the LUB and GLB of any 2 sets equals the intersection and the
union of those 2 sets respectively.
We need to find LUB(A, B) which is the intersection of A and B - therefore the LUB(A, B) will
contain all the multiples of 24 (as LCM(8, 12) = 24) upto 256. Similarly LUB(B, C) will contain all
the multiples of 36 which are less than or equal to 256. Finally the LUB of these two will contain
all multiples of 72 which are less than or equal to 256. Hence we get the set {72, 144, 216}. Therefore
the answer will be equal to 3.

DIV: R5, R3, R4

Q61 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following resource allocation graph of a system with P0, P1, P2 and P3 processes, and R0, R1, R2 and R3 resources.

 
 
Small circle in rectangle shows instance of that resource. So, R0, R1 and R3 have single instances and R2 has two instances. Consider the following statements for above resource allocation graph of system:
S1 : System is in deadlock.
S2 : Cycle in resource allocation graph is sufficient to detect deadlock in system with multiple instance for resources.
S3 : If process “P1” from above system is aborted/terminated and release its allocated resources, then system will be free from deadlock.
Which of above statements is/are correct?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Cycle is necessary, but not sufficient condition for deadlock in multi-instance resource system.
In above graph, no process able to complete its execution without its required resource. So, it is in deadlock.
If P1 is aborted, then system will remain in deadlock state. Because, it does not hold any resource.
If any process other than P1 get aborted then system will be deadlock free.
